sector.name: Co-Working
sector.description: Managed workspace operators serving enterprises and GCCs

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,493 Cr (+24% YoY); co-working and allied services revenue stood at ₹1,237 Cr (+35% YoY)
Operating EBITDA stood at ₹550 Cr (+37% YoY), with operating EBITDA margin of 37%
PAT before exceptional items stood at ₹71 Cr (+66% YoY)
Normalized EBITDA stood at ₹213 Cr (+27% YoY), with normalized EBITDA margin of 14%
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹410 Cr (+21% YoY); Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹23 Cr (+107% YoY)

guidance.value: ₹130 Cr under delivery for next 6-7 months

highlights: FY26 was a defining year marked by strong execution, enterprise adoption, premiumization and capital discipline
Awfis is focusing on 5 growth engines: premiumization, multi-format supply, GCC demand, organic growth and adjacencies
100% of new supply signed in FY26 was in Grade A / A+ assets
Partial Managed Office model is gaining traction, where 40-70% seats are already anchored at signing stage
GCC business now has 100+ unique GCC clients across 9 cities and contributes around 23% of rental revenue
Awfis Transform third-party revenue grew from ₹95 Cr in FY25 to ₹152 Cr in FY26
AWFIS Space Solutions Ltd.quote: "Awfis is outperforming the sector on capital efficiency, ROCE, GCC traction, premiumization and asset-light managed aggregation model."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,037 Cr (+58% YoY); EBITDA stood at ₹468 Cr (+43% YoY)
FY26 PAT stood at ₹235 Cr (+67% YoY), with PAT margin of 23%
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹293 Cr (+39% YoY); Q4FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹144 Cr (+32% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹69 Cr (+45% YoY), with Q4 PAT margin of 24%
Leasing revenue stood at ₹536 Cr (+44% YoY), Design & Build revenue at ₹438 Cr (+66% YoY), and Furniture revenue at ₹63 Cr (+202% YoY)
EFC (I) Ltd.outlook: EFC is positioning itself as an integrated real estate-as-a-service platform across Leasing, Design & Build and Furniture. Working capital efficiency and collections are key priorities for FY27, while Furniture is viewed as margin-accretive backward integration.
highlights: Leasing provides recurring revenue and client stickiness, while Design & Build provides execution capability and growth momentum
Leasing business has presence across 25 cities and serves 750+ clients across multiple sectors
Average enterprise client tenure is around 51 months
Top 10 client contribution reduced to around 24%, showing lower concentration risk
Design & Build has 80+ designers and engineers, 45+ reputed clients and design footprint around 5.5 mn sq ft
Furniture business has a 1.2 lakh sq ft manufacturing facility in Pune, 1,500+ SKUs and 60,000+ delivered units
EFC (I) Ltd.quote: "EFC is outperforming the sector on PAT growth, high PAT margin, integrated model, D&B scaling and Furniture-led backward integration."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,469 Cr (+37% YoY)
EBITDA crossed ₹300 Cr (+60% YoY), with EBITDA margin of 21% vs 18% last year
PAT stood at ₹125 Cr (+145% YoY), with PAT margin of 9% vs 5% last year
Cash flow from operations stood at ₹304 Cr (+147% YoY)
Added 1.6 mn sq ft rent-paying area and launched 15 new centers in FY26
highlights: FY26 was a landmark year with record revenue, margin expansion and strong cash generation
Area under management stood around 9.66 mn sq ft; rent-paying area stood around 7.84 mn sq ft
Average occupancy for FY26 stood around 84%, while steady-state occupancy improved to 88%
Value-added services contribution increased to 15% vs 12% last year and is expected to move to 17-18%
Solar investments are expected to generate ₹22-23 Cr annual savings
GCC demand remains strong across BFSI, healthcare, logistics, manufacturing and global services
Indiqube Spaces Ltd.quote: "IndiQube is outperforming the sector on revenue growth, PAT growth, margin expansion, solar-led cost savings and VAS scale-up."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood around ₹1,800 Cr (+31% YoY)
Normalized EBITDA stood at ₹314 Cr (+75%+ YoY), with normalized EBITDA margin of 17.5%
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹520 Cr (+45% YoY and +10% QoQ)
Q4FY26 normalized EBITDA stood at ₹99 Cr (+71% YoY), with normalized EBITDA margin of 19%
FY26 was the first full year of Ind AS PAT profitability and net debt turned negative by ₹56 Cr
highlights: Company crossed 10 mn sq ft operational area in Q4FY26
Contracted rental revenue crossed ₹5,200 Cr, with signed annuity-like contracts from large enterprises
Contracted revenue locks in 82.5% of FY27 revenue
Enterprise revenue remains above 90% and average tenure is over 44 months
Overall occupancy stood at 82%, impacted by around 1 mn sq ft new operational area in Q4
1,000+ seat cohort contributed 37% of rental revenue vs 29% last year
Smartworks Coworking Spaces Ltd.quote: "Smartworks is outperforming the sector on contracted revenue visibility, enterprise concentration, operational scale, margin expansion and ROCE improvement."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹2,477 Cr (+23% YoY), while revenue from operations stood at ₹2,454 Cr (+24% YoY)
Core operations revenue stood at ₹2,077 Cr (+23% YoY); VAS revenue stood at ₹295 Cr (+36% YoY)
EBITDA stood at ₹499 Cr (+23% YoY), with EBITDA margin of 20%
PAT stood at ₹179-180 Cr (+134% YoY)
Free operating cash stood at ₹586 Cr (+44% YoY); net debt turned negative at ₹12 Cr
highlights: FY26 was the strongest year in WeWork India's history across occupancy, revenue, EBITDA, PAT and cash
Company has 76 centers, 8.6 mn sq ft operational area and 11.6 mn sq ft AUM
Portfolio occupancy improved from 76.8% to 86.9%, while mature center occupancy stood at 88.9%
FY26 desk sales stood close to 48,000 desks, equivalent to 3.3 mn sq ft leasing
77% of core revenue comes from enterprises and 65% comes from global members headquartered outside India
FY27 opens with ₹1,885 Cr of core revenue already locked in
Wework India Management Ltd.quote: "WeWork India is outperforming the sector on occupancy, cash generation, enterprise mix, pricing power, net cash position and ROCE."
